Average sleep duration supports progressive overload.

body.note: Height retained from WHOOP (not in InBody CSV). Weight/composition from InBody 2026-07-16.

goals.primary: Look better with a stronger, more defined upper body
goals.notes: Upper-body bias locked for Plan C block. Visual: bigger shoulders, more definition, less belly. Legs maintenance only.

status.label: detrained_restart
status.note: No structured training for ~2–3 months; plan design deferred until source packs exist.

philosophy: Fewer longer gym sessions over many short ones
Weekly flexible slots — not fixed clock times
Stable exercise menu; progress via load/reps
Home fallbacks required when gym is impossible

gym.equipment: Full commercial gym (free weights, machines, cables, cardio)

nutrition.self_assessment: Mostly under control; not asking for a full diet overhaul
suspects: Carbs may be low outside breakfast
nutrition.creatine: 5 g/day when training (paused during gym lapse; resume with restart)
nutrition.tracking: No perpetual food logging — spot-checks only if needed for calibration

sports_optional: Paddle — not reliable enough to prescribe

history_timeline.note: Gym membership after cleaning up junk-food eating; somewhat consistent
Restart with friend; researched splits; consistency hard under schedule pressure
No structured training ~2–3 months; occasional home work historically; creatine paused
InBody baseline ingested; source-pack strategy next — plan still locked

measurement.inbody: Baseline on file (2026-07-16); prefer ~monthly — cadence pending sourced guidance
measurement.home_scale: TBD usefulness after sourced retest guidance

today.local_hint: Open Today on your phone at the gym. Gym first. Home only if gym is impossible.

guided_workout.title: Recovery / off day
guided_workout.summary: No lifting prescribed today (MWF plan). Walk, sleep, hit protein + creatine.

habits.hydration: Drink consistently; pale urine — more on training days / heat
habits.eating_frame: Maintenance or tiny surplus on training days; do NOT hard-cut (BF% already ~17%). Soft midsection improves more from muscle + consistency than aggressive deficit in 3 weeks.

guided_workout.missed_day_rule: Priority: (1) Gym on the planned Mon/Wed/Fri. (2) If you miss one, still aim for 3 gym days that week on other weekdays. (3) Home fallback only if gym is impossible. Weekends only if that is the only way to keep a gym session.
attendance_priority: First prize: Gym on Monday, Wednesday, and Friday
Second: Still three gym days — makeup Tuesday or Thursday if you miss a plan day
Last resort: Home only if getting to Virgin Active is truly impossible for that session

plan.headline: Plan C · 3×75 min · Mon/Wed/Fri · ease-in restart

block.label: Plan C — 3-week ease-in restart
preference_notes: Upper-body visual priority: shoulders, definition, less belly
Home fallbacks: pull-up bar, rope, bodyweight
First gym return in months — week 1 is deliberately easier

visual_primary: Less belly / softer midsection (recomp, not crash cut)

conservative.percent_body_fat: hold or −0.3 to −0.8 pp (may be within BIA noise)
conservative.smm_kg: hold or +0.2 to +0.5 (small; 3 weeks is short)
conservative.note: Conservative = process + small composition drift in the right direction

optimistic.shoulders_subjective: Visible rounder delts in mirror / shirts
optimistic.note: Optimistic = best-case if adherence + protein + sleep hold; InBody noise still applies (pack 07)

process_non_negotiable: Do Mon/Wed/Fri (home swap counts if gym impossible)
Week 1 leave reps in reserve — do not ego-load

daily_habits.hydration: Drink consistently; pale urine — more on training days / heat
daily_habits.eating_frame: Maintenance or tiny surplus on training days; do NOT hard-cut (BF% already ~17%). Soft midsection improves more from muscle + consistency than aggressive deficit in 3 weeks.

1.label: Ease-in
1.load_cue: Start ~60–70% of what you remember. Smooth reps. Stop if form breaks.
1.reps_in_reserve: Leave about 2 to 3 reps in reserve (you could still do 2–3 more clean reps). Last set can finish closer to 2 left.

2.label: Build
2.load_cue: Add load or reps vs week 1 when top of range feels solid.
2.reps_in_reserve: Leave about 1 to 3 reps in reserve.

3.label: Push (still controlled)
3.load_cue: Full Plan C volume. Progress load if week 2 was clean.
3.reps_in_reserve: Leave about 1 to 2 reps in reserve on isolation moves; about 2 on big compound lifts.

push.focus: Chest, front/side delts, triceps · small leg maintenance

gym.notes: Don't shrug. Full lockout without slamming.

gym.name: Dumbbell or cable side lateral raise
gym.notes: Visual priority — soft elbows, lead with elbows.

gym.name: Cable or machine chest fly
gym.notes: Squeeze at the inside; no ego weight.

home.notes: Same minutes as gym finisher for this week.

shoulders.focus: Side delts volume · visual priority day · light legs + core

Dumbbell or cable side lateral raise
Incline dumbbell or barbell press

plan.missed_day_rule: Priority: (1) Gym on the planned Mon/Wed/Fri. (2) If you miss one, still aim for 3 gym days that week on other weekdays. (3) Home fallback only if gym is impossible. Weekends only if that is the only way to keep a gym session.
notes: Week 1 = ease-in (fewer sets, leave more reps in reserve). Do not chase old personal records.
Open portal Today tab in the gym — toggle Gym vs Home.
Creatine 5 g daily · protein ~115–130 g · no hard cut.
InBody re-test target: Sat 2026-08-08 (or next weekday if easier). Matched conditions (pack 07).
Golf stays bonus only — not a planned session.
attendance_priority: First prize: Gym on Monday, Wednesday, and Friday
Second: Still three gym days — makeup Tuesday or Thursday if you miss a plan day
Last resort: Home only if getting to Virgin Active is truly impossible for that session

session_tips.title: Best time of day
session_tips.body: For muscle size over weeks, morning and evening training are about equal when weekly hard sets are matched. A 2019 meta-analysis (Grgic et al., Chronobiology International) found similar hypertrophy morning vs evening (effect size near zero). Pick the time you will actually arrive — that beats the “perfect” clock.

session_tips.title: Food around training
session_tips.body: Daily protein (~115–130 g for you) matters more than a magic 30-minute window. For this restart: a small meal or snack 1–2 hours before usually feels better than going completely empty (less light-headedness). If you train fasted and feel fine, that is OK — just hit protein across the day (ISSN protein position stand).

session_tips.title: How hard (reps left in reserve)
session_tips.body: Week 1: stop each set when you could still do about 2–3 more clean reps. You do not need every set to failure. Training near failure is enough for growth; grinding every set to failure is not required (failure vs non-failure reviews).

session_tips.title: Weights in kilograms
session_tips.body: Virgin Active plates and dumbbells are in kilograms. Write the number on your phone notes: Set 1 = X kg. Next set, try the same or +2.5 kg if it felt easy.

session_tips.title: Showing up beats perfect
session_tips.body: First prize: gym Monday / Wednesday / Friday. Second: still three gym days with a makeup weekday. Home only if the gym is truly impossible.

overall_tips.title: Best time of day
overall_tips.body: For muscle size over weeks, morning and evening training are about equal when weekly hard sets are matched. A 2019 meta-analysis (Grgic et al., Chronobiology International) found similar hypertrophy morning vs evening (effect size near zero). Pick the time you will actually arrive — that beats the “perfect” clock.

overall_tips.title: Food around training
overall_tips.body: Daily protein (~115–130 g for you) matters more than a magic 30-minute window. For this restart: a small meal or snack 1–2 hours before usually feels better than going completely empty (less light-headedness). If you train fasted and feel fine, that is OK — just hit protein across the day (ISSN protein position stand).

overall_tips.title: How hard (reps left in reserve)
overall_tips.body: Week 1: stop each set when you could still do about 2–3 more clean reps. You do not need every set to failure. Training near failure is enough for growth; grinding every set to failure is not required (failure vs non-failure reviews).

overall_tips.title: Weights in kilograms
overall_tips.body: Virgin Active plates and dumbbells are in kilograms. Write the number on your phone notes: Set 1 = X kg. Next set, try the same or +2.5 kg if it felt easy.

overall_tips.title: Showing up beats perfect
overall_tips.body: First prize: gym Monday / Wednesday / Friday. Second: still three gym days with a makeup weekday. Home only if the gym is truly impossible.

incline_press.where: Adjustable bench + dumbbells (or barbell / Smith incline). Bench about 30–45 degrees.
incline_press.how_to: Set the bench to about 30–45 degrees. Plant your feet flat. Pull your shoulder blades back into the pad. Start with the weights at upper-chest height, elbows about 45 degrees from your torso (not flared flat out). Press up until arms are almost straight without slamming. Lower for about 2 seconds until you feel a stretch across the upper chest.
incline_press.avoid: Bench too upright (turns into a shoulder press). Bouncing the bar/dumbbells off the chest. Huge arch and lifting the hips.
incline_press.set_plan: Set 1: pick a weight (X kilograms) you can do for 8–10 reps with about 2–3 reps still left. Set 2: same X kg, or X + 2.5 kg if set 1 felt easy. Set 3 (when you have 3 sets): try X + 2.5 kg only if form stayed smooth — otherwise stay at X.
tips: Remember: control the lowering — that stretch is where a lot of the chest work happens.
Remember: keep shoulder blades pinned; do not shrug into your ears.
If you only find a barbell incline machine, use that — same idea.
incline_press.research_note: Weekly hard sets on a muscle drive growth more than which machine you pick (Schoenfeld volume dose-response). Upper-chest emphasis from incline is a programming choice for your visual goals — not a magic percentage over flat press.

shoulder_press.display_name: Seated dumbbell or machine shoulder press
aliases: Seated dumbbell or machine shoulder press
shoulder_press.where: Seated bench with back support + dumbbells, or a shoulder-press machine.
shoulder_press.how_to: Sit tall with your back against the pad. Start with dumbbells at about ear height. Press overhead until arms lock softly. Lower back to ear height. Do not shrug. On a machine: same path, slow and controlled.
shoulder_press.avoid: Big lower-back arch. Pressing the weights far out in front of your face.
shoulder_press.set_plan: Set 1: X kilograms for 8–10 reps with 2–3 reps left. Set 2: X or X + 2.5 kg. Set 3 (if scheduled): same rule — only add 2.5 kg if set 2 was clean.
tips: Remember: this builds the front and middle of the shoulder — key for that round look.
Remember: stop the set when form shakes; do not fight ugly reps in week 1.
shoulder_press.research_note: Shoulders respond to local weekly volume. Extra hard sets here support your visual shoulder goal without needing a fourth gym day.

lateral_raise.display_name: Dumbbell or cable side lateral raise
aliases: Dumbbell or cable side lateral raise
Cable or machine side lateral raise
lateral_raise.where: Dumbbells standing, cable with a handle at your side, or a lateral-raise machine.
lateral_raise.how_to: Soft bend in the elbows. Lead with the elbows and raise arms out to just below shoulder height. Pause briefly. Lower slowly. Prefer clean reps over heavy weight — this is your main “wider shoulders” move.
lateral_raise.avoid: Swinging the body. Shrugging traps. Flinging weights above the head.
lateral_raise.set_plan: Set 1: light X kilograms (often 4–8 kg to start) for 12–15 reps with 2 reps left. Set 2: same X, or +2 kg / +2.5 kg if easy. Later sets: stay strict — if you need swing, the weight is too heavy.
tips: Remember: stop just below shoulder height if traps take over.
Remember: side delts are a priority for looking broader in shirts — quality beats ego weight.
lateral_raise.research_note: Specialty volume on a region works because hypertrophy is largely local (sets per muscle). Raising week-to-week volume here is a standard specialization approach.

chest_fly.where: Pec-deck / fly machine, or dual cables at chest height.
chest_fly.how_to: Keep a slight bend in the elbows and keep that bend fixed. Open until you feel a chest stretch, then bring the arms together as if hugging a tree. Squeeze one second at the inside.
chest_fly.set_plan: Set 1: X kilograms (or machine pin) for 12–15 smooth reps. Set 2: same or + one pin / +2.5 kg if easy.
tips: Remember: this is a control / pump move — light is correct.
Remember: feel the stretch; do not bounce.
chest_fly.research_note: Isolation work adds local volume. It does not replace your main presses — it tops up chest work inside the time budget.

aliases: Barbell or DB curl + pushdown supersets optional
triceps_pushdown.where: Cable stack with a rope or straight / angled bar.
triceps_pushdown.how_to: Pin elbows to your sides. Push down until arms straighten. If using a rope, spread the ends apart at the bottom. Let the forearms come up only as far as the elbows stay still.
triceps_pushdown.avoid: Leaning and turning it into a chest press. Elbows flaring wide.
triceps_pushdown.set_plan: Set 1: X kilograms for 10–15 reps with 2 reps left. Set 2: X or X + 2.5 kg.
tips: Remember: only the forearms should move — elbows stay glued.
Remember: full straighten at the bottom for the triceps squeeze.
triceps_pushdown.research_note: Arms get work from presses too; this finishes triceps without long sessions.

leg_press_or_goblet.display_name: Leg press or goblet squat (or walking lunges)
aliases: Walking lunges OR leg press light
Leg press or goblet squat (or walking lunges)
leg_press_or_goblet.where: Leg-press sled, or one dumbbell for goblet squat, or open floor for walking lunges.
leg_press_or_goblet.how_to: Leg press: feet mid-platform, lower until thighs are about 90 degrees, press without slamming the locks. Goblet squat: hold one dumbbell at your chest, sit between your heels, stand tall. Lunges: short steps, torso upright. These are maintenance only — leave 2–3 reps in reserve.
leg_press_or_goblet.avoid: Butt peeling off the leg-press pad. Knees collapsing inward.
leg_press_or_goblet.set_plan: Set 1: X kilograms for 10–12 controlled reps. Set 2: same X (do not chase a leg personal record this block).
tips: Remember: legs stay in the plan for health balance (WHO major-muscle guidance) — not to become a leg specialty.
Remember: stop early if knees hurt; choose the machine that feels best.
leg_press_or_goblet.research_note: Young adults can maintain muscle with much lower volume than they used to build it (Bickel et al., 2011). A few hard sets keep the floor without stealing time from upper body.

aliases: Jump rope OR marching in place
Jump rope OR brisk walk outdoors
conditioning.where: Stair climber, incline treadmill, or jump-rope space.
conditioning.how_to: Easy-moderate pace where you can still talk in short sentences. Stair climber: light hands on the rails, full steps. Incline walk: about 5–8% incline, brisk walk not a sprint. Jump rope: steady rhythm. Use the minutes shown on the card.
conditioning.avoid: All-out intervals that trash tomorrow’s lift. Hanging on the stair climber rails and cheating the steps.
conditioning.set_plan: One block of easy cardio for the minutes listed. No “X kilograms” — just steady effort.
tips: Remember: this supports conditioning and midsection goals; lifting still builds the shoulders.
Remember: if you are exhausted from lifting, shorten this, do not skip the whole gym day.
conditioning.research_note: Extra easy cardio is optional support. Your main belly/definition lever in this 3-week restart is lifting + protein + not hard-cutting, not punishing cardio.

lat_pulldown.where: Lat-pulldown station, assisted pull-up machine, or a pull-up bar.
lat_pulldown.how_to: Pulldown: start with a long reach, pull elbows toward your hips, bar to upper chest, control the return. Assisted pull-up: same idea with help from the knee pad. Negatives: get the chin over the bar, lower for 3–5 seconds.
lat_pulldown.avoid: Pulling the bar behind the neck. Huge swinging half-reps.
lat_pulldown.set_plan: Set 1: X kilograms (pulldown stack) for 8–10 reps with 2–3 left. Set 2–3: X or X + 5 kg if the stack jumps by 5 and form stays clean.
tips: Remember: think “elbows to hips,” not “hands to bar.”
Remember: full stretch at the top every rep.
lat_pulldown.research_note: Back volume supports posture and the V-taper that makes shoulders look wider.

seated_row.where: Seated cable row with a V-handle or straight bar.
seated_row.how_to: Sit tall with a slight knee bend. Start with arms long. Pull the handle to your torso, squeeze the shoulder blades, do not shrug. Return to a full stretch. Keep the torso mostly still.
seated_row.avoid: Rocking with the lower back. Shrugging the traps every rep.
seated_row.set_plan: Set 1: X kilograms for 8–10 reps. Set 2–3: X or X + 2.5–5 kg if clean.
tips: Remember: chest proud, shoulders down.
Remember: squeeze at the chest for one second.
seated_row.research_note: Rows balance pressing days and help shoulder health with your pressing volume.

rear_delt.display_name: Rear delt fly (dumbbell, cable, or machine)
aliases: Rear delt fly (dumbbell, cable, or machine)
rear_delt.where: Bent-over dumbbells, reverse pec-deck, or cables.
rear_delt.how_to: Soft elbows. Sweep arms out and slightly back — show the rear of the shoulders to the wall behind you. Light weight; feel the burn.
rear_delt.avoid: Turning it into a heavy shrug or row with swing.
rear_delt.set_plan: Set 1: light X kilograms for 12–15. Set 2–3: same or +2 kg / +2.5 kg.
tips: Remember: this rounds out the shoulder from the back — important for the 3D look.
Remember: if you only feel traps, go lighter.
rear_delt.research_note: Rear-delt work supports aesthetics and pressing comfort.

face_pull.where: Cable set at upper-chest / face height with a rope.
face_pull.how_to: Pull the rope toward your face / forehead. At the end, rotate so thumbs point roughly toward your ears. Squeeze rear delts and upper back.
face_pull.avoid: Pulling the rope to the belly (that becomes a regular row).
face_pull.set_plan: Set 1–2: light X kilograms for 12–15 smooth reps.
tips: Remember: this is shoulder-friendly “prehab” and shape work — form first.
Remember: external rotation at the end matters.
face_pull.research_note: Helpful accessory for shoulder longevity while you bias pressing for visuals.

curl.how_to: Elbows near your sides. Curl up without swinging the hips. Lower fully. Brief squeeze at the top.
curl.set_plan: Set 1: X kilograms for 10–12. Set 2–3: X or X + 2 kg / +2.5 kg.
tips: Remember: lower all the way — skipping the bottom chops the set short.
Remember: if you swing, drop the weight.
curl.research_note: Direct arm work is optional volume on top of rows/pulldowns.

rdl_or_leg_curl.display_name: Romanian deadlift or lying leg curl
aliases: Romanian deadlift OR lying leg curl
Romanian deadlift or lying leg curl
rdl_or_leg_curl.where: Barbell or dumbbell Romanian deadlift, or lying/seated leg-curl machine.
rdl_or_leg_curl.how_to: Romanian deadlift: soft knees, push hips back, weights close to the legs, feel hamstrings stretch, stand by driving hips forward. Leg curl: pad on Achilles, curl heels toward glutes, control down. Light–moderate — hinge maintenance.
rdl_or_leg_curl.avoid: Rounding the upper back hard. Yanking the leg curl.
rdl_or_leg_curl.set_plan: Set 1: X kilograms for 8–12. Set 2: same X (maintenance).
tips: Remember: hips go back like closing a car door with your hips.
Remember: this is not a max deadlift day.
rdl_or_leg_curl.research_note: Keeps posterior-chain coverage for the WHO-style major-muscle floor while upper body stays the priority.

core_crunch.display_name: Cable crunch or hanging knee raise
aliases: Cable crunch OR hanging knee raise
Cable crunch or hanging knee raise
core_crunch.where: Kneeling cable crunch, or pull-up bar for hanging knee raise.
core_crunch.how_to: Cable crunch: kneel, rope at shoulders or behind head, crunch ribs toward pelvis — do not yank with arms. Hanging knee raise: dead hang, tip the pelvis, raise knees toward the chest under control.
core_crunch.avoid: Swinging like a gymnast kip. Pulling the rope with arms only.
core_crunch.set_plan: Set 1–2: controlled 10–15 reps (cable pin = X kilograms as a guide).
tips: Remember: think “ribs to pelvis,” not “pull the rope.”
Remember: abs show more from body fat + overall training than from endless crunches alone.
core_crunch.research_note: Core work is fine for feeling and control; your midsection look still depends mostly on overall fat loss rate and genetics — and you are already near ~17% body fat.

elevated_pushup.how_to: Hands under shoulders (closer together for a diamond push-up). Body straight. Lower the chest toward the hands, press up. Hands elevated = easier. Feet elevated = harder.
elevated_pushup.set_plan: Home sets: match the set count on the card. Soften difficulty with a higher hand elevation rather than ugly form.
tips: Remember: body in one line from head to heels.
Remember: this is the home stand-in for pressing.
elevated_pushup.research_note: Home volume counts when gym is impossible — still second-best to the gym priority ladder.

pike_pushup.how_to: Hips high in an inverted V. Bend elbows and lower the head toward the floor between the hands, then press back up. This copies a shoulder-press pattern at home.
pike_pushup.avoid: Letting the hips drop into a normal push-up.
pike_pushup.set_plan: Strict reps; elevate feet later only if easy.
tips: Remember: hips stay high.
Remember: this is your home shoulder builder.
pike_pushup.research_note: Home shoulder pattern when dumbbells are not available.

bw_squat.how_to: Feet about shoulder width. Sit down and slightly back, knees track over toes, stand tall. Split squat: one foot forward, drop the back knee toward the floor.
bw_squat.avoid: Heels lifting. Knees caving hard inward.
bw_squat.set_plan: Smooth controlled reps for the sets listed.
tips: Remember: this is only leg maintenance at home.
Remember: hold a backpack for load if bodyweight is too easy.
bw_squat.research_note: Minimum lower-body stimulus when you cannot reach the gym.

plan_adherence.interpretation: whoop_logged=true means some WHOOP workout exists that calendar day — not proof the exact lifts were done. Use with Dylan's report / portal.

trust.message: Plan C cites data/sources/01–07. Follow the portal session; ask Cursor for adjustments.
